Failing test: "should bring a dead cell to life when it has exactly three living neighbors (reproduction)"
Expected behavior: A dead cell with exactly 3 living neighbors becomes alive in the next generation
Current error: AssertionError - dead cell at [1,0] not produced as alive in next generation
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-03_13-10-14_game-of-life-user-story_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

Implement reproduction: dead cells with exactly 3 living neighbors become alive. You'll need to consider all neighbor positions of living cells as candidate cells for reproduction.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state and implement the minimal code to make the reproduction test pass.

Now I understand. The reproduction test expects that a dead cell at [1,0] (which has 3 living neighbors at [0,0], [2,0], [1,1]) becomes alive.

Minimal implementation: I need to consider candidate dead cells (neighbors of living cells) and check if they have exactly 3 living neighbors.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass (1 todo remaining).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added reproduction logic by computing candidate dead cells (neighbors of living cells, deduped, excluding already-living) and including those with exactly 3 living neighbors in the next generation.
- Result: All tests now pass (7 passing, 1 todo)
- Approach: Minimal - reused existing `countLivingNeighbors` helper. The candidate dead cells helper iterates through neighbors of living cells using a Set to dedupe, which is the simplest correct approach to satisfy the test.
